concept: Modular rad-tolerant GaN PPU brick for small-satellite electric propulsion
named_buyer_examples: Rocket Lab Space Systems (merchant components buyer/channel)
painful_job: Every EP integrator re-develops and re-qualifies a bespoke PPU per thruster generation because no standard architecture has emerged after 50+ years (L09-001..L09-008, L09-011); small satellites are priced out of electric propulsion by PPU cost/mass, which is exactly the gap the JAXA/Furukawa co-development program exists to close (L09-042), and NASA has paid $67M (AEPS) and $18.4M (NEXT-C) for bespoke PPU chains (L09-034, L09-012).
product: Flight-qualified 300-1,500 W GaN-based anode-discharge power brick (28-100 V bus input, 200-600 V regulated discharge output) with integrated ignition sequencing, breathing-mode-tolerant current control, heater/keeper/magnet auxiliaries, and a parallelable current-share bus - a merchant PPU building block for Hall and gridded-ion thrusters.
frontier_vision: Become the standard merchant power layer of electric propulsion, from 300 W smallsats to orbital-transfer vehicles, where high-power PPUs are built by paralleling the same qualified brick instead of new NRE per program.
extreme_edge: Paralleled 50-100 kW PPU arrays for nuclear-electric and high-power SEP tugs.
first_experiment: Build one 500 W engineering-model anode brick on rad-tolerant GaN FETs; demonstrate ignition and stable discharge control against a laboratory Hall thruster in a university vacuum facility; run pulsed-laser SEE pre-screen plus one cyclotron spot-check at the chosen voltage derating.
technical_mechanism: High-frequency (500 kHz-1 MHz) GaN phase-shifted full-bridge/LLC stage with planar magnetics gives a 3-5x mass/volume reduction versus silicon-era PPUs; a digital controller handles ignition transients, wide output range (L09-008), and damping of 10-100 kHz discharge (breathing-mode) oscillations; single-event-transient immunity via voltage-clamp mitigation (L09-108) and heavy-ion-informed voltage derating of GaN/SiC devices (L09-103, L09-104); interfaces designed to ECSS-E-ST-20-20C and AIAA S-122 (L09-111, L09-115).
why_incumbents_miss: US incumbents (Aerojet Rocketdyne/L3Harris, Moog) are organized around bespoke cost-plus NASA PPU contracts, and Aerojet now sits inside a missile-focused parent where space EP is a minor line item (L09-041, L09-043); component vendors (VPT, EPC Space) sell generic rad-hard DC-DC converters, not thruster-aware discharge supplies with ignition/oscillation control (L09-053, L09-054, L09-055).
nearest_substitutes: In-house PPUs at Busek/Moog/Mitsubishi Electric; VPT rad-hard converter assemblies (L09-045, L09-053); EPC Space GaN converter/controller parts as build-blocks (L09-054, L09-055); the Furukawa/JAXA GaN Hall-thruster supply if it commercializes on schedule (L09-042).
uncertainty: Whether small-sat EP builders buy a merchant PPU rather than protect in-house NRE, and whether GaN survives SEE qualification at useful derating without falling back to a SiC/Si hybrid output stage.
founder_fit_note: Generic EE/CE transfer: power-converter circuit design, embedded control loops, and prototyping/DAQ skills map directly onto building and bench-validating a digitally controlled discharge supply.

concept: Hall-thruster electrical emulator: power-hardware-in-the-loop discharge load for PPU qualification
painful_job: PPU teams in Japan, China, the US, and India each burn scarce vacuum-facility time and coupled-test schedule to debug PPU-thruster electrical interaction because topologies have not converged (L09-001, L09-002); ISRO's 1,000-hour life test (L09-035) and NEXT-C's build-the-prototype-PPU-first sequencing (L09-012) show how expensive and serialized the current test path is.
product: Rack-mounted 600 V / 2 kW (parallelable to 20 kW) bidirectional power-hardware-in-the-loop load that reproduces Hall/ion-thruster electrical behavior - ignition inrush, negative-resistance V-I regions, 10-100 kHz breathing-mode oscillation spectra, and fault/plume-impedance steps - so PPU developers run qualification-grade electrical campaigns without vacuum-chamber time.
frontier_vision: Every PPU and thruster program pre-qualifies electrically against the emulator before vacuum test, making it the de facto electrical reference for EP; the accumulated fault/aging waveform library becomes the industry's thruster digital-twin asset.
extreme_edge: Full-mission electrical digital twin including thruster wear-out signatures, used for on-orbit EP health management.
first_experiment: Implement a 600 V/10 A wide-bandgap PHIL stage driven by an FPGA real-time discharge model; validate that it reproduces published SPT-100-class discharge-current spectra, ignition transients, and mode transitions within ~10% against literature waveforms.
technical_mechanism: A fast bidirectional converter (>1 MHz small-signal bandwidth) is commanded by a real-time 0-D plasma model (breathing-mode oscillator plus empirical V-I maps from published thruster characterizations, L09-004..L09-006) so the PPU under test sees the same terminal impedance, transients, and anomalous modes as a firing thruster - including scripted end-of-life behavior derived from life-test literature (L09-109).
why_incumbents_miss: Test-equipment majors see too small a market; thruster houses treat discharge dynamics as internal IP and have no incentive to productize a tool that helps competitors; the required knowledge spans plasma physics and power electronics, which sit in different organizations at primes.
nearest_substitutes: Coupled vacuum-facility testing (status quo, L09-035); generic electronic load banks that cannot present negative-resistance discharge dynamics; one-off in-house emulators at large labs.
uncertainty: Total buyer population is likely tens of labs/vendors worldwide, so this is a niche instrument business unless it becomes the referenced pre-qualification step in EP power standards.
founder_fit_note: Generic EE/CE transfer: real-time embedded modeling, fast power-stage design, and instrumentation/numerical-analysis skills are exactly what a power-hardware-in-the-loop emulator requires.

concept: Pulsed-laser single-event-effects screening station for wide-bandgap space power devices
painful_job: GaN/SiC space qualification is unresolved - destructive single-event burnout appears near 50% of rated voltage and TID/displacement-damage synergies are non-additive (L09-014, L09-020, L09-103, L09-104) - and new 2025-2026 rad-tolerant GaN parts ship with no third-party validation (L09-055); every derating decision currently waits on scarce, expensive cyclotron beam time, even as NASA pays SBIR money for new rad-hard power ASICs that will all need SEE screening (L09-112, L09-113).
product: Turnkey two-photon-absorption pulsed-laser SEE screening station with high-voltage (to 1.2 kV) biased fixtures for GaN/SiC power transistors and converter boards, plus a curated derating database correlating laser thresholds to published heavy-ion data.
frontier_vision: The default pre-beam screening step and derating database for every wide-bandgap part flown - effectively the underwriting laboratory for space power semiconductors.
extreme_edge: Wafer-level inline laser SEE screening inside rad-hard fab process development.
first_experiment: Assemble a single-channel femtosecond TPA bench with a 600 V biased test fixture; screen one SiC MOSFET and one GaN HEMT type that already have published heavy-ion SEB/SEGR data and demonstrate laser-threshold-to-LET correlation adequate for derating decisions.
technical_mechanism: Sub-bandgap femtosecond pulses focused through the die backside deposit charge by two-photon absorption at controlled depth/position, triggering the same parasitic bipolar turn-on and avalanche paths as heavy ions (mechanisms per L09-104 for SiC, L09-103 for GaN); scanning the biased die maps SEB-sensitive regions and safe operating voltage without beam time; calibration transfers via reference devices with known ion response.
why_incumbents_miss: Heavy-ion beamlines are national facilities, not product companies, and are booked months out; part vendors cannot credibly self-certify; laser-SEE know-how lives in a few government groups and has never been packaged as a supported commercial instrument for kV-class power devices.
nearest_substitutes: Cyclotron/heavy-ion campaigns (the gold standard); laser-SEE services at national facilities; conservative blanket derating with mass/efficiency penalties.
uncertainty: Laser-to-ion correlation fidelity for destructive high-voltage power-device events is scientifically live; demand may concentrate in fewer than ten vendor/agency accounts.
founder_fit_note: Generic EE/CE transfer: instrumentation, precision measurement automation, and device-physics-literate data analysis transfer directly to building a laser-based semiconductor screening instrument.

concept: Rad-tolerant cryocooler drive electronics module for proliferated IR constellations
named_buyer_examples: NASA (SBIR precedent buyer, via Creare award topic)
SDA missile-tracking primes (Lockheed Martin, L3Harris, Northrop - EP/cooler content inferred, flagged)
painful_job: NASA is paying SBIR money for 'low cost radiation-hardened cryocooler control electronics' (L09-112) because flight cryocooler drives are bespoke, heavy, and a disproportionate share of cooler cost; proliferated missile-tracking constellations ($3.5B SDA Tranche 3 across four primes, L09-039) multiply cooled-IR payload counts, and merchant space components are a proven business model (Rocket Lab 10-K, L09-114).
product: A 50-300 W radiation-tolerant sinusoidal motor-drive and active vibration-cancellation controller for space cryocoolers (pulse-tube/Stirling): GaN output stage, 100 krad TID, SET-clamped control, exported-vibration suppression, delivered as a standard qualified module to cooler OEMs and payload integrators.
frontier_vision: Standard drive electronics inside every proliferated-constellation IR cooler, expanding to space quantum payload cryogenics and zero-boil-off cryogenic plants.
extreme_edge: Rad-hard drives for zero-boil-off cryo depots and nuclear-mission cryogenics.
first_experiment: Drive a commercial tactical pulse-tube cooler with a GaN sine-drive prototype; demonstrate exported-vibration cancellation to flight-representative levels and take the controller through 100 krad TID with SET laser spot-checks.
technical_mechanism: Dual half-bridge GaN stage synthesizes low-distortion 30-120 Hz sinusoidal drive for the linear compressor; accelerometer feedback drives a narrowband adaptive controller cancelling exported vibration harmonics (critical for IR pointing); SET hardening via voltage clamping (L09-108) and a TMR supervisor; GaN device derating set from heavy-ion literature (L09-103); control heritage transferable from free-piston Stirling convertor control (L09-105).
why_incumbents_miss: Cryocooler OEMs treat drive electronics as a bundled cost center, not a product; rad-hard power houses (VPT) sell DC-DC bricks, not motor control with vibration cancellation; the niche falls between thermal and power organizations at primes.
nearest_substitutes: Cooler-OEM in-house drives; Creare's SBIR-funded electronics if productized (L09-112); oversized legacy silicon rad-hard drives (L09-053-class parts).
uncertainty: Cooler/EP content of SDA constellations is not publicly confirmed (L09-039) - the volume assumption rests on inference; OEM bundling may block merchant module sales.
founder_fit_note: Generic EE/CE transfer: motor-drive power electronics, embedded control, and vibration/sensor signal processing are core EE/CE competencies applied to a compact drive module.

concept: High-voltage neutron-tolerant SiC PMAD bricks for fission surface power
named_buyer_examples: Lockheed Martin / BWXT / Creare FSP team
IX (Intuitive Machines/X-energy) with Maxar and Boeing
painful_job: NASA/DOE's Fission Surface Power spec jumped from 40 kWe to >=100 kWe (closed Brayton) within one program cycle, with White House NSTM-3 backing (L09-031 vs L09-033), stranding Phase 1 power-architecture baselines (Westinghouse's continuation still describes 40 kWe, L09-032, L09-047); every team needs power management and distribution that re-scales without redesign, and radiation and temperature tolerance must be qualified separately (L15-020).
product: A 1,000 VDC / 25 kW modular PMAD brick family - SiC solid-state breaker plus isolated DC-DC plus telemetry - rated for combined neutron/TID exposure and 150-200 C coldplate operation, so 40-100 kWe space-reactor teams scale PMAD by paralleling qualified bricks instead of redesigning.
frontier_vision: The PMAD backbone for surface reactors, nuclear-electric tugs, and lunar DC microgrids - the grid hardware of cislunar industrialization.
extreme_edge: Megawatt-class NEP distribution at kilovolt bus voltages.
first_experiment: Build one 1 kV/25 kW SiC brick; demonstrate sub-10-microsecond solid-state fault interruption and >96% conversion efficiency at 150 C coldplate; irradiate gate-drive and controller sections to program-representative neutron fluence plus 300 krad TID at a university research reactor.
technical_mechanism: Series-capable 1.2 kV SiC MOSFET stages with desaturation-based solid-state circuit breaking; LLC isolation with nanocrystalline planar magnetics; margining informed by measured neutron-induced SiC leakage degradation (L15-020) and SiC SEB physics (L09-104); high-temperature operation leans on SiC's demonstrated 500 C device headroom (L15-009); bricks share a current-share/telemetry bus so four bricks serve 100 kWe and two serve 40 kWe.
why_incumbents_miss: Reactor primes focus on core and Brayton conversion; spacecraft power houses focus on 100 V-class buses; kV-class neutron-tolerant PMAD falls between them, and the moving 40-to-100 kWe spec punishes bespoke designs while rewarding modular merchant bricks.
nearest_substitutes: Prime in-house PMAD development; Moog spacecraft power electronics heritage (L09-043); ad hoc requalification of terrestrial SiC converters.
uncertainty: Single-customer government program with political schedule risk (NSTM-3 dates can slip); neutron-tolerance data for kV-class SiC assemblies is thin; pre-flight revenue is paid engineering-model work only.
founder_fit_note: Generic EE/CE transfer: high-voltage converter design, protection circuits, and controls/telemetry engineering transfer directly to modular power-distribution hardware.

concept: Robotic megawatt-charging connection cell for autonomous battery haul trucks
named_buyer_examples: BHP and Rio Tinto (Jimblebar autonomous BEV trial)
painful_job: Fortescue had to build its own 6 MW charger (L10-029) while ordering up to 400 driverless-capable 240 t trucks (L10-028), and BHP/Rio/Cat are explicitly testing BEV integration with autonomous fleet management (L10-030) on fleets that are already autonomous at scale (L15-042) - but a driverless truck has no operator to mate a megawatt-class connector, and no productized automated MCS coupler exists for mining dust/vibration.
product: A vision-guided, dust-sealed robotic coupling cell implementing the IEC TS 63379 MCS interface (up to 1,250 V / 3,000 A) that automatically mates a 240-tonne autonomous battery haul truck to a multi-megawatt charger: +/-150 mm capture envelope, wear-cartridge contacts, contact-resistance and thermal monitoring feeding the charger derating loop.
frontier_vision: Lights-out energy exchange for all autonomous heavy fleets - mines, ports, rail yards - built on one robotic interface family.
extreme_edge: Fully unmanned mines where no human ever touches a megawatt connector.
first_experiment: Build a subscale automated coupler (500 kW-class contact set) on an industrial robot; run 1,000 mate/demate cycles in a dust chamber with injected misalignment; measure contact-resistance drift and thermal margin against IEC TS 63379 limits.
technical_mechanism: Fiducial-plus-lidar pose estimation guides a 6-DOF arm carrying a floating-compliance MCS coupler; spring-loaded wiping contacts in a replaceable cartridge absorb wear; embedded RTDs and milliohm-level contact monitoring allow safe operation at 3,000 A-class currents per the MCS envelope (L10-050); IP66 sealing with positive-pressure purge handles pit dust; the cell exposes a fleet-management API so autonomous dispatch (the workflow being trialed at Jimblebar, L10-030) treats charging as just another autonomous task.
why_incumbents_miss: Charger makers focus on power conversion and truck OEMs on vehicles, so the connection robot is orphaned interface engineering; the MCS standard only published in February 2026 (L10-050); mining OEM instincts (trolley, swap) bypass rather than solve static megawatt coupling.
nearest_substitutes: Manual connection by pit personnel (defeats autonomy); Liebherr trolley/'Power Rail' dynamic charging (L10-052); SPIC-style battery swap (L10-031); Cat MEC500 plug-in charging for smaller machines (L10-053).
uncertainty: Charging-architecture non-convergence: if swap or trolley wins at the 240 t class, static robotic coupling shrinks to a niche; Fortescue Zero or OEMs may in-house the coupler.
founder_fit_note: Generic EE/CE transfer: embedded systems, sensing/perception integration, and high-current electrical interface engineering cover the robotic coupler's core disciplines.

concept: Trolley-assist retrofit power kit for underground battery LHD loaders
named_buyer_examples: underground hard-rock mine operators running battery LHDs (Cat R1700 XE user base)
painful_job: Underground battery LHDs cannot complete a full production shift on battery alone, forcing mid-shift charge stops or swaps that break production (L10-014); trolley-assisted charging shows up to 75% simulated energy-cost reduction but remains unproductized research (L10-024); the incumbent answer is <20-minute fast-charge stops with two MEC500 chargers (L10-053), which still halts the machine.
product: A 300-400 kW drift-rail collector plus vehicle-side bidirectional SiC converter kit that lets battery-electric underground loaders draw tramming power and opportunity-charge on haul segments - eliminating mid-shift charging - delivered as an OEM-agnostic retrofit with a mine-side rectifier skid tied to existing 1 kV distribution.
frontier_vision: Electrified underground haulage networks where ramps and drifts are powered corridors and diesel-sized ventilation capex is designed out of new mines.
extreme_edge: Deep, hot mines (2-3 km) where diesel is thermally and ventilation-prohibitive.
first_experiment: Acquire or instrument one LHD duty cycle; bench-build the 300 kW collector-converter chain against an emulated 750 VDC mine rail; validate shift-completion and battery-degradation predictions of the published trolley-assist models on hardware.
technical_mechanism: A sprung dual-shoe collector on the drift back-rail feeds an isolated SiC DC-DC that blends rail power with the traction bus under a supervisory controller prioritizing regen capture on down-ramps (configuration space mapped by L10-014/L10-024); galvanic isolation and creepage design meet mine electrical codes; the rectifier skid connects to existing mine 1 kV distribution so no new primary infrastructure is required.
why_incumbents_miss: OEMs monetize proprietary chargers and batteries (the MEC500 ecosystem, L10-053) and draw their scope boundary at the machine; trolley expertise lives with surface-mining and rail suppliers who do not serve underground headings; retrofit economics are too small for majors but material for mid-tier mines.
nearest_substitutes: Battery swap bays; more/faster chargers (L10-053); larger onboard batteries at payload penalty; Chinese swap-standard ecosystems if exported underground (L10-046).
uncertainty: Evidence is simulation-heavy - no multi-year fleet economics exist (lane caution) - and per-mine electrical-code approval could dominate deployment cost.
founder_fit_note: Generic EE/CE transfer: power-converter design, energy-management control, and ruggedized embedded instrumentation are the retrofit kit's core engineering content.

concept: Relocatable containerized trolley-assist substation for open-pit haul routes
named_buyer_examples: Hitachi Construction Machinery / ABB trolley-hybrid deployments (Kansanshi lineage)
painful_job: Liebherr's T264 BEV is trolley-ready with a claimed 86% energy-cost/tonne reduction (L10-052) and Hitachi/ABB's flagship BEV runs battery-trolley hybrid (L10-054), but conventional trolley substations are fixed civil works - a poor match to pits whose ramps move every few years; stranded-infrastructure fear is a documented reason miners have not converged on trolley despite buying trolley-capable trucks (L10-028, L10-029).
product: A 3-6 MW containerized rectifier substation (SiC active front end; 11-33 kV mine loop in, 1.8-3 kV DC catenary out) engineered for jack-and-roll relocation in days, with pre-terminated cabling, integral reels, and modular mast/catenary sections - infrastructure that moves with the pit.
frontier_vision: Movable pit electrification as a product category: power infrastructure that follows the orebody instead of anchoring it.
extreme_edge: Fully trolley-electrified 100-truck pits with zero permanent substations.
first_experiment: Build a 250 kW SiC active-front-end demonstrator of the substation architecture; measure harmonics/flicker against mine power-quality limits under simulated truck-entry/exit load steps from published haul-cycle profiles; produce a certified relocation time-and-cost validation on a mock pad.
technical_mechanism: A SiC active front end regulates catenary voltage under 2-3 MW load steps as trucks enter and leave the wire (dynamics per trolley-configuration studies L10-002, L10-015), with a supercapacitor buffer smoothing grid draw so weak mine networks qualify; the container integrates protection, cooling, and pre-terminated connections so relocation is a crane lift plus a recommissioning checklist rather than new civil works.
why_incumbents_miss: Grid-equipment vendors sell permanent substations sized by utility norms; truck OEMs draw their boundary at the pantograph; nobody owns 'infrastructure that moves with the pit', which is mining-specific systems engineering rather than a bigger transformer.
nearest_substitutes: Fixed trolley substations from ABB-lineage suppliers (L10-054); static megawatt charging farms (L10-029); battery swap architectures (L10-031).
uncertainty: Trolley uptake beyond a few flagship sites is unproven, and miners may insist on bundling substations into OEM trolley packages.
founder_fit_note: Generic EE/CE transfer: power electronics, grid power-quality analysis, and control-system engineering transfer directly to a converter-centric substation product.

concept: Automated wayside megawatt charging mast for battery-electric locomotives
named_buyer_examples: BHP / Rio Tinto / Roy Hill / CN battery-locomotive programs
painful_job: UP, Vale, BHP, Rio Tinto, Roy Hill, and CN have ordered FLXdrive battery locomotives now reaching 7.0 MWh per unit (L10-032, L10-033, L10-051), but corridor studies show benefits hinge on where and how fast batteries are recharged (L10-010, L10-011, L10-012), and no standardized multi-megawatt wayside charging product exists for heavy-haul yards - the road-truck MCS standard (Feb 2026) has no rail adaptation (L10-050).
product: A yard-installed overhead charging mast with an automated inverted-pantograph contact head delivering 1-3 MW (MCS-derived power core per IEC TS 63379) to battery locomotives during crew changes and yard dwell, with dispatch-integrated charge scheduling so no timetable changes are needed.
frontier_vision: A charging-mast network that lets battery locomotives run full corridors, then serves battery road trains and port shuttles from the same hardware family.
extreme_edge: 10 MW-class five-minute boost charging of 7+ MWh locomotives.
first_experiment: Prototype the automated contact head at 350 kW on a fixture emulating locomotive roof geometry and yard misalignment; demonstrate 5,000 automated engage/disengage cycles outdoors in dust/rain; validate charge-window sufficiency against published corridor duty cycles.
technical_mechanism: A rising mast with an inverted pantograph locates on locomotive roof contact rails; actively cooled MCS-grade contact pairs carry 1-3 MW within the IEC TS 63379 electrical envelope (L10-050); the power core is a series-stacked SiC AFE plus isolated DC-DC fed from 13.8/25 kV yard supply; a scheduling controller fits charging into existing dwell events identified in corridor modeling (L10-010..L10-012).
why_incumbents_miss: Wabtec sells locomotives and treats charging as the railroad's problem (L10-051 lists no charging product); railway electrification vendors think in catenary-miles, not point charging; the MCS standards ecosystem is road-truck oriented, leaving rail adaptation unowned.
nearest_substitutes: Charging batteries in-consist from diesel units (status quo hybrid operation); discrete catenary segments; depot chargers if Wabtec productizes them (L10-051).
uncertainty: Battery-rail order flow is still pilot-scale (trials-not-fleet-economics caution); Wabtec verticalization risk; railroad capital-approval cycles are slow.
founder_fit_note: Generic EE/CE transfer: high-power conversion, electromechanical actuation control, and embedded scheduling software are standard EE/CE capabilities applied at rail scale.

concept: Diesel-RTG hybrid conversion kit monetizing awarded Clean Ports grants
named_buyer_examples: EPA Clean Ports grant-holding US port authorities and terminals (53 awards)
painful_job: EPA has already awarded nearly $3B across 53 Clean Ports grants for zero-emission port equipment (L10-048) - obligated IRA money that survives the CARB drayage repeal - and terminal operators show category appetite by ordering 53 electrified RTGs at once (L10-041) and signing port-equipment electrification MOUs with CATL (L10-036); yet thousands of existing diesel RTGs are too young to scrap and too dirty to keep, and new-crane replacement is the only productized path.
product: A retrofit kit converting diesel rubber-tired gantry cranes to battery-hybrid: 600 kW bidirectional SiC DC-bus converter, LTO pack sized to capture hoist-lowering regeneration, genset downsizing controller, and optional shore-cable input - installable in under two weeks per crane.
frontier_vision: The default mid-life electrification path for the global installed base of tens of thousands of diesel RTGs and yard cranes, extending to reach stackers and straddle carriers.
extreme_edge: Fully grid-tied zero-genset terminals running on the same DC-bus kits.
first_experiment: Datalog one operating diesel RTG's power profile for two weeks; bench-validate the 600 kW converter plus LTO pack capturing the measured hoist-regen profile; deliver a bankable per-crane fuel/maintenance savings model to one grant-holding terminal.
technical_mechanism: The kit inserts a common DC bus between the existing genset alternator and hoist drives; lithium-titanate chemistry tolerates 200+ deep micro-cycles per day from hoist regeneration that today is burned in brake resistors; the supervisory controller peak-shaves so a roughly half-size genset (or shore cable where available) covers average load - the same energy architecture Konecranes sells in new E-Hybrid cranes (L10-041), delivered as a retrofit.
why_incumbents_miss: Crane OEMs prefer selling new electrified cranes (L10-041); battery majors chase vessel and fleet-scale deals (L10-036); mixed-OEM 15-year-old crane retrofits are fragmented integration work incumbents deprioritize - but grant spending deadlines create urgency now.
nearest_substitutes: New E-Hybrid RTG purchases (L10-041); full busbar/cable-reel lane electrification; CATL-partnered OEM offerings (L10-036).
uncertainty: Federal grant administration and politics could slow disbursement; per-site engineering variance may erode kit margins.
founder_fit_note: Generic EE/CE transfer: power-flow datalogging/DAQ, converter design, and battery-management control are the whole retrofit engineering stack.

concept: TR136-compliant swappable battery module and quayside cabinet for Singapore harbour craft
named_buyer_examples: Singapore harbour-craft operators (crew transfer, cargo supply) under MPA's 2030 mandate
Singapore yards building compliant new craft
painful_job: MPA requires all new harbour craft in the Port of Singapore to be fully electric, B100-capable, or net-zero-fuel-compatible from 2030, and published its TR136 charging/battery-swap safety standard only in March 2025 - under five years of runway for hundreds of small operators (L10-055); China's canal fleet proves 15-minute swap operations work at vessel scale (L10-040) and classification rules for battery ships are in force (L10-045), but no TR136-native swap product exists.
product: A crane-handled, standardized ~250 kWh LFP battery module with TR136-aligned blind-mate HV connector, thermal-runaway containment, and coded mechanical keying, plus a quayside charge-and-swap cabinet where one cabinet serves 4-6 craft on staggered swaps.
frontier_vision: The swap-module standard for Southeast Asian small-vessel electrification - harbour craft, tugs, ferries, fishing fleets - with Singapore as the regulatory reference market.
extreme_edge: Autonomous harbour craft exchanging batteries at unmanned swap pontoons.
first_experiment: Build one module plus cabinet prototype; run a certification gap-analysis against TR136 with a Singapore certification body; demonstrate 100 crane swap cycles and a thermal-runaway containment test on the module casing.
technical_mechanism: IP67 module with blind-mate HV connector and coded mechanical keying following battery-swap-system standards practice (L10-046); vent channeling and mist suppression contain single-cell thermal runaway per classification-rule expectations (L10-045); the cabinet recharges at 0.5C with grid demand management, decoupling vessel turnaround (minutes) from charging time (hours) exactly as demonstrated by China's swap vessels (L10-040).
why_incumbents_miss: CATL-scale players target large vessels and fleet MOUs (L10-036, L10-040); Singapore's fragmented small-operator segment is too small for majors and too capital-intensive for local yards, and TR136 is too new for incumbent-compliant products to exist.
nearest_substitutes: Fixed quayside DC charging under TR136; the B100 biofuel compliance path permitted by the same mandate (L10-055); ad hoc adaptation of Chinese swap systems (L10-040).
uncertainty: The mandate binds new builds only, so fleet-turnover speed sets adoption; MPA infrastructure-execution risk; the B100 escape route may siphon compliance demand.
founder_fit_note: Generic EE/CE transfer: battery-system electronics, safety interlock/embedded control design, and standards-driven test engineering map onto the swap module and cabinet.

concept: 225 C IC obsolescence-bridge chipset on merchant SOI (CISSOID/Honeywell replacement)
named_buyer_examples: Welltec and PetroQuip (FORGE awardees building 225 C tools)
Baker Hughes ($600M R&D, $2.0B New Energy orders)
downhole MWD/LWD and intelligent-completion OEMs stranded by CHT last-time-buy
painful_job: CISSOID marked its established -55 C to +225 C CHT IC family obsolete/last-time-buy while itself positioning as the replacement for Honeywell's exited high-temperature line (L15-043) - stranding downhole and geothermal tool OEMs with no qualified second source just as DOE FORGE pours $93M into 225 C-class tools (L15-027, L15-028) and Baker Hughes books $2.0B of New Energy orders including 300 MW of geothermal ORC equipment (L15-040).
product: Pin/function-compatible replacements for the discontinued 225 C catalog - starting with gate driver, precision op-amp, and voltage reference - fabricated on a current merchant 180 nm SOI foundry process, ceramic-hermetic packaged, shipped with published 1,000 h / 250 C HTOL data and a 10-year availability guarantee.
frontier_vision: The trusted merchant source for extreme-environment ICs, expanding from 225 C bridge parts to 300 C-native families as superhot geothermal and aero-engine demand hardens.
extreme_edge: Venus-surface-class (465 C) instrumentation ICs in the NASA HOTTech lineage (L15-030).
first_experiment: Tape out the gate driver plus op-amp on a merchant 180 nm SOI multi-project-wafer run; assemble in ceramic packages with AuGe die-attach; run 1,000 h HTOL at 250 C and publish parameter-drift data against CHT datasheet limits.
technical_mechanism: Partially-depleted SOI with buried oxide suppresses the junction leakage that kills bulk CMOS above ~150 C, retaining analog functionality to ~300 C (L15-004); a high-temperature design kit (leakage-compensated biasing, dielectric-only capacitors, electromigration-derated metallization) reproduces the proven Deep Trek/Honeywell SOI approach (L15-031) on a live foundry process; AuGe or sintered-silver die-attach with hermetic ceramic packaging addresses the documented packaging failure layer (L15-005, L15-025).
why_incumbents_miss: Honeywell exited because downhole volumes are small next to its aerospace core; CISSOID is tied to an obsolete fab line; merchant SOI foundry processes now make small-volume 225 C parts economical in a way they were not when Deep Trek ran - a supply-shock wedge, not a growth story incumbents chase.
nearest_substitutes: Last-time-buy inventory hoards of CHT parts; CISSOID's remaining CMT/CXT lines (L15-043); redesigning tools around 175 C automotive-grade parts plus active cooling dewars.
uncertainty: True annual unit volume for 225 C ICs is opaque (no vendor pricing, L15-043); the double obsolescence could reflect genuinely shrinking demand rather than stranded demand.
founder_fit_note: Generic EE/CE transfer: analog/mixed-signal circuit design, device characterization, and reliability test instrumentation are the direct skill set for a small IC product line.

concept: 300 C-native downhole data-acquisition module for superhot geothermal wells
painful_job: ARPA-E SUPERHOT targets >375 C reservoirs (10-20 GW potential, L15-029) and DOE FORGE has spent $93M pushing 225 C tools (L15-027, L15-028), while the commercial downhole electronics ceiling remains 225 C with no vendor roadmap past it (L15-043 and the lane's explicit no-roadmap finding) - so EGS players signing real contracts today (SLB-Ormat pilot L15-041; Baker Hughes 300 MW ORC award L15-040) face a widening gap between drilling capability and electronics survivability.
product: A cooling-free 300 C-rated downhole instrumentation module: SiC-JFET analog front end, 300 C SOI digitizer/controller, SiC piezoresistive pressure and temperature sensing, packaged as a drop-in sub for logging and monitoring strings in EGS and superhot wells - no dewar, therefore no bottom-hole time limit.
frontier_vision: The standard instrumentation platform for superhot geothermal - the sensing layer that unlocks >375 C baseload resources - then permanent in-well monitoring arrays.
extreme_edge: Permanent 400 C in-well arrays and Venus-lander instrumentation reuse (L15-030 lineage).
first_experiment: Assemble an analog front end around available SiC JFET die (NASA Glenn lineage) plus 300 C-capable SOI ADC evaluation silicon; soak-test the instrumented module at 300 C / 30 MPa in an autoclave for 500 h while streaming data through a high-temperature cable head.
technical_mechanism: SiC JFET ICs (demonstrated 500 C / 1-year operation at NASA Glenn, L15-009) buffer and multiplex sensor signals at ambient well temperature; a 300 C SOI ADC/controller (L15-004) digitizes downhole so only robust digital telemetry travels uphole; SiC piezoresistive pressure die (L15-006, L15-007) replace oil-filled capillary gauges; eliminating the vacuum-flask dewar removes the exposure-time limit that defines today's tool operations (contrast the active-cooling literature, L15-023, L15-024).
why_incumbents_miss: SLB/Baker Hughes portfolios are engineered around oilfield 150-200 C with dewars for excursions, and superhot geothermal volume is too small and uncertain to redirect their platform roadmaps - which is why two decades of federal programs (L15-031, L15-037, L15-026) never produced a purchasable 300 C platform.
nearest_substitutes: Dewared conventional electronics with time-limited runs (L15-023, L15-024); memory-only gauges retrieved after cooldown; 225 C designs pushed with derating (L15-043).
uncertainty: SUPERHOT award flow and superhot well count set a small near-term market; drilling-side bottlenecks (bits, casing, fluids - L15-021) could gate demand regardless of electronics readiness.
founder_fit_note: Generic EE/CE transfer: sensors/instrumentation, data-acquisition electronics, and harsh-environment circuit prototyping are the module's core disciplines.

concept: 300 C-junction SiC power module platform - packaging as the product
named_buyer_examples: downhole tool and geothermal drilling OEMs (Baker Hughes-class)
DARPA THERMAL-adjacent performers needing packaged deliverables
painful_job: Four independent 2024-2026 reviews still identify die-attach/packaging as the unresolved limiting factor even where SiC die tolerate the temperature (L15-005, L15-025, L15-015, L15-007), and the one merchant high-temp vendor has marked its flagship IC family obsolete (L15-043) - integrators funded by SUPERHOT/THERMAL-era programs (L15-029, L15-026) cannot buy a qualified >250 C power module anywhere.
product: A qualified half-bridge SiC power module family rated for 250-300 C junction operation - pressure-assisted silver-sintered die-attach on active-metal-brazed Si3N4 substrates, organics-free encapsulation, hermetic option - sold with a published qualification dossier to integrators stranded above the 175-225 C ceiling of commercial modules.
frontier_vision: The qualified packaging layer for all >250 C power electronics: downhole, aero-engine, reactor-adjacent, and eventually Venus-class systems.
extreme_edge: 600 C-junction SiC modules at the theoretical device ceiling (L15-001).
first_experiment: Build 20 half-bridge modules with the sintered-Ag/AMB stack; run 500 thermal-shock cycles (-55 C/+300 C) plus 1,000 h high-temperature gate bias at 300 C junction; publish acoustic-microscopy delamination and on-resistance drift data as the qualification dossier.
technical_mechanism: Pressure-assisted silver sintering produces a die-attach whose melting point (~960 C) removes the first failure layer of soldered modules; Si3N4 AMB substrates CTE-match SiC to survive deep thermal cycling; polyimide-free encapsulation or hermetic lids eliminate organics that pyrolyze above 250 C - directly attacking the failure modes catalogued by the packaging reviews (L15-005, L15-025); die sourced from merchant SiC supply chains including open R&D lines (L15-039).
why_incumbents_miss: Automotive module giants qualify to 175 C because that is where their volume is; SiC device startups spend capital on die, not packaging lines; a public 300 C qualification dossier is slow, unglamorous evidence that two decades of federal programs never converted into a purchasable product (L15-026, L15-031).
nearest_substitutes: CISSOID's remaining 1200 V modules at lower temperature ratings (L15-043); in-house hybrid assemblies at primes; active-cooling workarounds (L15-023).
uncertainty: Qualification must be partially re-earned per customer mission profile, and >250 C-capable die supply (gate-oxide reliability) may cap the practical junction rating.
founder_fit_note: Generic EE/CE transfer: power-device electrical characterization, thermal/electrical co-design, and automated reliability testing transfer to a packaging-qualification product.

concept: Teleoperation/drive-by-wire retrofit cell for ancillary open-pit equipment
named_buyer_examples: Baogang Steel Union (live RMB3.75M 5G remote-control retrofit tender)
painful_job: Autonomous haulage is industrially mature for trucks (Cat/Rio Tinto's 20-truck, 4-drill Koodaideri order, L15-042; active 2026 literature, L15-018), but ancillary machines - dozers, graders, rollers, water carts - still put operators in dust, blast zones, and highwall risk; buyers are paying now: Baogang tendered ~RMB3.75M just for phase-two 5G remote-control retrofit of dozers/rollers within an ~RMB18M program (L10-035).
product: An OEM-agnostic retrofit kit - sealed electrohydraulic actuators or CAN-level integration, dual-LTE/5G plus mesh comms, safety PLC with geofence and loss-of-comms auto-stop, and a remote operator station that multiplexes 3-6 machines - converting ancillary pit equipment to teleoperation with supervised-autonomy hooks.
frontier_vision: The full ancillary-fleet autonomy layer for open pits worldwide - the second wave after truck autonomy - sold per machine plus site license.
extreme_edge: Zero-human-in-pit operations including dozing on active highwalls.
first_experiment: Retrofit one used dozer with the actuator/comms kit on a closed pad; demonstrate an 8-hour production-representative teleoperation shift including latency budget, loss-of-comms failsafe validation, and operator workload measurement across two multiplexed machines.
technical_mechanism: Machine-dependent bolt-on electrohydraulic actuators or CAN message injection execute controls; a safety PLC enforces geofencing, obstacle-stop from solid-state lidar/radar, and watchdogged comms with graceful degradation; deterministic-latency video over bonded 5G/LTE links mirrors the architecture in the Baogang tender scope (L10-035); one operator supervising several intermittent-duty machines is the economic core.
why_incumbents_miss: Cat Command and OEM autonomy stacks monetize flagship truck fleets at flagship mines (L15-042); mixed-fleet ancillary retrofit is low-glamour, per-machine-variant integrator work OEM product organizations avoid - yet it is exactly what SOE tenders are buying today (L10-035).
nearest_substitutes: Single-OEM remote-control options; Cat Command scope expansion (L15-042); keeping human operators in cabs.
uncertainty: Safety-case and standards acceptance per jurisdiction (the mining-autonomy ISO standard text was not publicly verifiable in the evidence base); Western-entrant access to Chinese SOE tenders likely requires a local partner.
founder_fit_note: Generic EE/CE transfer: embedded systems, control engineering, and communications/latency-aware system integration are the retrofit cell's central skills.

concept: Dose-managed sacrificial electronics cartridge for nuclear-decommissioning robotics
named_buyer_examples: Japan's Fukushima Daiichi decommissioning ecosystem (Quince lineage institutions)
UK/EU decommissioning agencies whose cost case is already published
painful_job: Fukushima-class deployments gamma-test off-the-shelf robot electronics ad hoc before every campaign (Quince, L15-011; Beihang, L15-012), and UK analysis shows planned replacement of standard components can beat specialized rad-hard design on cost (L15-002) - but no product embodies that philosophy, so each program re-runs Co-60 campaigns and improvises maintenance with no dose accounting.
product: A quick-swap 'sacrificial cartridge' for decommissioning robots: COTS compute, motor drives, and cameras in a spot-shielded, blind-mate module with an onboard dosimeter and prognostics that schedule replacement before dose-induced failure - sold as cartridge subscriptions instead of rad-hard part premiums.
frontier_vision: The consumable-electronics model for all radiation-heavy robotics: decommissioning, fusion in-vessel maintenance, and isotope-production plants.
extreme_edge: Reactor-vessel-interior robotic fleets sustained on cartridge logistics.
first_experiment: Package a Jetson-class compute plus motor-drive cartridge with an onboard dosimeter; irradiate five units to failure under Co-60 to build the dose-to-failure distribution; demonstrate sub-5-minute hot-swap replacement on a mock manipulator in a glovebox mockup.
technical_mechanism: Selective tungsten-copper spot shielding around the most dose-sensitive parts (flash, regulators) plus TMR-protected FPGA supervision (L15-022) extends COTS electronics life 3-10x; an onboard dosimeter drives a prognostic model that converts the UK planned-replacement cost argument (L15-002) into an enforceable maintenance schedule; blind-mate connectors and captive fasteners make swap a manipulator-safe task; device-level TID/SEE behavior bounded by published GaN/SiC and COTS degradation data (L15-013, L15-014).
why_incumbents_miss: Rad-hard component vendors profit from selling $1,500-2,100 parts (L09-053-class pricing), not from making cheap parts disposable; robot OEMs lack irradiation-test access and treat electronics as static BOM; a consumables business model is foreign to both sides.
nearest_substitutes: Full rad-hard electronics stacks (L09-053-class); per-program ad hoc COTS gamma testing (L15-011, L15-012); tethered robots that keep electronics outside the field.
uncertainty: Buyers are a handful of slow government programs and the sub-lane is pilot-stage by the lane's own adjudication - revenue timing is the dominant risk.
founder_fit_note: Generic EE/CE transfer: embedded compute/motor-drive integration, dosimetry instrumentation, and prognostics from test data are standard EE/CE capabilities for this cartridge.
